title: Velvety Vietnamese Cookies Mi Quang
description: A creamy, single-serving Vietnamese-inspired soup that melts crunchy cookies into a velvety broth with fragrant lemongrass and coconut milk.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add 2 tsp vegetable oil until shimmering.
Add the smashed lemongrass, sliced ginger, whole star anise, and 1 tsp turmeric powder; sauté for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in 500ml water and 400ml coconut milk, then bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Stir in 1 tsp salt, 1 tbsp sugar, and the torn lime leaf until the sugar dissolves.
Reduce the heat to low, and gradually add the crushed shortbread cookies, stirring constantly until they melt into a velvety broth.
Continue to simmer for 10 minutes at low heat, allowing the flavors to meld and the cookies to fully dissolve.
Remove the skillet from heat and discard the lemongrass, ginger, star anise, and lime leaf pieces.
Ladle the soup into bowls, then sprinkle with 2 tbsp toasted sesame seeds as garnish.
Serve the hot, creamy soup immediately as a satisfying solo dinner.
